Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 231 [Ašipus’ Handbook][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢SAG⸣ EŠ₂.GAR₃ MAŠ.MAŠtu₄ ša₂ a-na iḫ-zu u ta-mar-tu₄ kun-nu nap-ḫa-ri MUar₂

(o 1) Incipits of the exorcism series which have been fixed for learning and consulting. The whole name(s).

o 22

⸢d⸣SIG₄ ⸢SUḪUŠ E₂⸣ [...][...] u ⸢ni⸣-šu-ut EN.NA

(o 2) 'Kulla' (gloss: to lay [...] the foundations of a house); [...] and 'Installation of a priest'.

o 33

⸢TU₆ ABZU GI⸣.NU.⸢TAG.GA⸣ u ŠU.LUḪ DINGIR.RA

(o 3) 'Incantation of Apsu'; 'Ginutaqqû' and 'Purification rituals of the gods'.

o 44

⸢KI⸣.d[UTU.KAM ŠU.IL₂].LA.KAM u DINGIR.ŠA₃.DIB.BA

(o 4) 'Kiʾutukku (Incantation by Šamaš)'; 'Šuʾillakku (Prayer of raised hands)' and 'Kimiltu (Divine wrath)'.

o 55

[ne₂]-peš ⸢iti⸣[ŠU ... iti]⸢KIN⸣ [iti]DU₆ u sak-ke-e LUGALu₂-ti₃

(o 5) Rituals of the months Duʾuzu, [...], Ululu (and) Tašritu and royal rituals.

o 66

[...] u KA.TA.DU₁₁.GAu₂

(o 6) [...] and 'Kataduqqû (Physiognomic omina)'.

o 77

[...] u [ḪUL].⸢BA.ZI⸣.ZI-MEŠ

(o 7) [...] and 'To set out the evil'.

o 88

[...] u ZI₃.[SUR].RA-MEŠ

(o 8) [...] and 'Magic circles'.

o 99

[...] u TU.RA ⸢KILIB₃⸣.BA

(o 9) [...] and 'All illnesses'.

o 1010

[...] ⸢u⸣ ALAN.NIG₂.E₂.SAG.IL₂.LA₂-MEŠ

(o 10) [...] and 'Substitute statues'.

o 1111

[... me-se]-⸢ri⸣-[MEŠ] u KA.LUḪ.U₃.DA [x?] DINGIR?u₂?

(o 11) [...] Bīt mēserī (House of confinement)' and 'Mīs pî (Mouth washing) [...] of the gods'.

o 1212

UŠ₁₁.⸢ḪUL⸣.[GAL₂-MEŠ ...] UŠ₁₁.BUR₂.⸢DA⸣ u NAM.ERIM₂.BUR₂.RU.DA

(o 12) 'Ušhulgallû (Evil sorcery)'; [...]; 'Ušburrudû (To dispel sorcery)' and 'Namerimburrudû (To remove a curse)'.

o 1313

KI.dUTU.[KAM₂ ... UŠ₁₁].BUR₂.RU.DA NAM.⸢ERIM₂⸣.BUR₂.RU.DAe DAB TU₁₅ u dDIM₃.ME

(o 13) 'Kiʾutukku (Incantation by Šamaš) [...]'; 'Ušburrudû (To dispel sorcery)'; 'Namerimburrudû (To remove a curse)'; 'Blast of wind' and 'Lamaštu'.

o 1414

⸢ḪUL⸣ ka-la [... šur]-pe-e MAŠ₂.[GE₆ ...] u ŠA₃.ZI.GA

(o 14) 'All evil'; [...]; 'Šurpu (Burning)'; '[...] dream [...]' and 'Sexual potency'.

o 1515

munus[PEŠ₄ ...] u lu₂TUR ḪUN.GA₂

(o 15) 'The pregnant woman [...]'; [...] and 'To appease a Baby'.

o 1616

IGI ⸢GIG⸣.[GA.KE₄ ...] u ⸢KA.ḪAB⸣ DAB.BA

(o 16) 'The sick eye'; [...] and 'The būšānu-disease seizure'.

o 1717

ŠA₃ ⸢GIG⸣.[GA.KE₄ ...] u TU₆.TU₆ GIG DU₃.A.BI

(o 17) 'The sick innards'; [...]; and 'Incantations against all illness'.

o 1818

UŠ₂ ⸢KIR₄⸣ [...] u ŠA₃.SUR KU₅.RU.DA

(o 18) '[...] the nosebleed'; [...] and 'To stop diarrhoea'.

o 1919

ZU₂ MUŠ ⸢TI⸣.[LA ...] u SAG.NIM.NIM TI.LA

(o 19) 'To cure a snake bite'; [...] and 'To cure samānu-disease'.

o 2020

GIR₃ ḪULti₃ ina ⸢E₂⸣ [... NAM].UŠ₂-MEŠ : šu-tu-qu u SISKUR₂ GABA.RI

(o 20) 'Foot of evil from a house of [...]' : 'To avert [...] plague' and 'To cause offerings to be received'.

o 2121

⸢ne₂-peš⸣ IRI E₂ A.ŠA₃ gišKIRI₆ ID₂ [...] x u gi₅-ne-e d⸢NISABA⸣

(o 21) Rituals of the city, house, field, orchard (and) canal [...] and 'Regular contributions to Nisaba'.

o 2222

U₄.DE₃.RA.RA DIB.BE₂.DA ZU₂ [...] u ⸢MAŠ!⸣.MAŠ x EDIN.NA

(o 22) 'To avert the rainstorm'; '[...] tooth' and 'Exorcist of the steppe'.

Reverse
r 1r 1

EDIN.NA DIB.BE₂.DA GI lu₂KUR₂ la₃ TE.GE₂₆.[E.DE₃] u KI-šu₂ AL.⸢DIB⸣

(r 1) 'To pass through the open country'; 'That an enemy arrow not approach' and 'Imprisonment'.

r 22

TUR₃ <AB₂>.GU₄-MEŠ U₈.UDU-[ḪI?.A?] u ANŠE.KUR.RA SIKIL.E.DE₃

(r 2) 'To purify the stalls of cattle, flocks and horses'.

r 33

EŠ.BAR MUL-MEŠ MUŠEN-⸢MEŠ⸣ [...] [MAŠ₂].⸢ANŠE⸣-[MEŠ] [...] ⸢NA₄⸣-MEŠ DUR₂.NA.DI₅ u DINGIR DU₃.A.BI

(r 3) '(Ominous) decisions from stars, birds, [...] (and) livestock'; '[...] of the ...-stones' and 'All the gods'.

r 44

NA₄ GARšu₂ U₂ GARšu₂ DUB NA₄-MEŠ DUB U₂-MEŠ tak-ṣi-ri u ma-la-lu

(r 4) 'Abnu šikinšu (Stone(s) whose appearance is)'; 'Šammu šikinšu (Plant(s) whose appearance is)'; 'Tablet(s) of stones'; 'Tablet(s) of plants'; 'Strings of amulet stones' and ...'.

r 55

⸢SAG⸣ x x EŠ₂.GAR₃ MAŠ.MAŠti₃ ⸢ša⸣ mEŠ₃.GU₂.ZI—GI—A DUMU ša₂ <m>dASAR.LU₂.ḪI—MA.AN.SUM

(r 5) Incipits of all the exorcism series of Esaggil-kin-apli, son of Asalluhi-mansum,

r 66

⸢ABGAL m⸣ḫa-am-mu—ra-bi LUGAL ⸢TIN⸣.TIRki ŠA₃.BAL.BAL m!li₉-si₄-a lu₂GUDU₄ E₂!.ZI.⸢DA⸣

(r 6) the sage of Hammu-rabi, king of Babylon, descendant of Lisia, pašīšu-priest of Ezida, for learning, reading and copying.

r 77

NIG₂.⸢ZU⸣.ŠE₃ IGI.DU₈.A DU-ma ⸢KIN⸣ [...] KI ma-la d[IDIM] ⸢ib⸣-ši-mu KID₃.KID₃ṭe₃-e ŠER₃.KU₃.⸢GA⸣-MEŠ1

(r 7) ... all the actions of [...] ... that [Ea] established; kikiṭṭu-rituals; šerkugû-songs.

r 88

NIG₂.KID₃.KID₃ NAM.⸢BUR₂.BI A₂⸣-MEŠ ANe u KIti₃ ma-la GAL₂a

(r 8) All existing magical procedures (and) namburbû-rituals (against) the portents from heaven and earth.

r 99

kul-lat na-⸢ag⸣-[bi ne₂]-⸢me⸣-qi₂ ni-⸢ṣir-tu₄⸣ KA.KU₃.GAL₂lu-ti

(r 9) The totality of wisdom (and) the secret of exorcism.

r 1010

[...] GIŠ.ḪUR-MEŠ AN u KI AD.ḪAL LAL₃.GAR u TU₆.TU₆ [BAR].⸢RA⸣

(r 10) [...] plans of heaven and earth, the secret of Lalgar and the non-canonical incatations.

r 1111

[... šim]-ma-tu₄ ri-mu-tu₄ SA.GAL SA.GIG ki-is-sa-tu₄ u KINti x x

(r 11) [...] šimmatu-paralysis, rimûtu-paralysis, sagullu-illness, 'maškadu-illness, kissatu-illness and ....

r 1212

[bul]-⸢ṭi⸣ AN.TA.ŠUB.BA dLUGAL—UR₃.RA ⸢ŠU⸣ DINGIR.RA ŠU dINANA u ŠU ⸢GIDIM.MA⸣

(r 12) Treatments against: falling sickness, Bel-uri-demon, Hand of a god, Hand of Ištar and Hand of a ghost,

r 1313

[A.LA₂] ⸢ḪUL LIL₂⸣.[LA₂].EN.NA KI.SIKIL.⸢LIL₂.LA₂⸣.EN.NA SAG.ḪUL.ḪA.ZA ŠU NAM.ERIM₂.MA u ŠU NAM.LU₂.U₁₈.LU

(r 13) evil alû-demon, lilû-demon, ardat-lilî-demon, Evil spirit, Hand of a curse and Hand of humanity,

r 1414

⸢u₃⸣ bul-⸢ṭi⸣ kal gim-ri ri-⸢kis TAG⸣it GIG KUM₂ DABsu u KIN MUNUS

(r 14) and the treatment against the whole range of affliction(s) of a sick person, against seizure of fever and against women's afflictions,

r 1515

⸢a-di⸣ rik-sat i-šip-pu-ti ta-⸢kaš⸣-ša₂-du tam-ma-ru NIG₂.URI₃

(r 15) until you obtain knowledge of the range of exorcism, you get to know the secret.

r 1616

ar₂-ka-na NIG₂.ZI.GAL₂ EDIN.NA ⸢INIM⸣.[BAL].⸢E⸣.DE₃ u EME.SAL-MEŠ

(r 16) Afterwards, you will interpret the commentaries (lit. the animals of the open country), the glosses and the compositions in Emesal,

r 1717

KI.DU.DUe EME.GI₇!(MEŠ) u ⸢EME⸣.[URIki] ši-te-ʾ-a ta-ḫi-za

(r 17) you will learn to research the rituals in Sumerian and Akkadian,

r 1818

⸢ZA₃⸣.GAR ZU.DE₃ GIM A.ZA.AD U₄.ŠU₂.⸢UŠ⸣.MA! * U₄ AN dEN.LIL₂.LA₂ u IRI ina SUKUD GARin

(r 18) in 'When one is to know the liver omen', '(The parts of the head) azad (and) ušuš', 'Enūma Anu Ellil (When Anu and Ellil)' and in 'Šumma ālu ina mēlê šakin (If a city is set on a height)',

r 1919

⸢kit-pu⸣-du!-ma šu-ta-ad-di-nu mit-ḫur-tu₄

(r 19) devise and exchange (for) an agreement.

r 2020

[A.MA].AL UR DUB.⸢LA₂.KE₄⸣ EREŠ₅ GI.BUR₃ DINGIR.DINGIR IŠIB!.IŠIB!.KAM? GIŠ.⸢TUG?⸣ DAGAL.LA SUM.MU

(r 20) ..., ..., wise, erudite, the gods of exorcism will give expanded intelligence.

r 2121

[... d]⸢LAMMA⸣-BI! ⸢ḪE₂.EN.SA₆⸣ [U₄].⸢UL⸣.DU₃.A.⸢ŠE₃⸣ MU-NE BA.AN.SA₄.A

(r 21) May [...], his tutelary god, be good! His name will be pronounced until distant days.

(colophon)
r 2222

[...] ki-i KA ṭup-pi GABA.RI SAR-ma IGI.KAR₂ AK.A [...] mri-mut—da-nu

(r 22) In accordance with the (original) tablet. Duplicate written and checked and properly executed. [...] Rimut-Anu

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

[... mdša]-maš₂—MU A lu₂⸢SANGA⸣—dMAŠ : UNUGki iti⸢DU₆?⸣ [...] mda-ri-ia-a-muš LUGAL

(t.e. 1) [...] Šamaš-iddin, descendant of Šangi-Ninurta. : Uruk, month Tašritu, [...], Darius the king.

1regarding the size of the break, the sign after {d} should be IDIM
